allowed_use: candidate date and tile selection review
editor reference thumbnails for guide/geospatial discussion

attached_thumbnails.visual_review_note: Clear lower/coastal tile; useful for Caribbean outlet color and lowland context, not sufficient by itself for the full rainforest gorge.
Inland/western companion tile with visible cloud scatter; useful for checking canopy and terrain texture leads, but not enough for mask authority.

forbidden_use: water, vegetation, canopy, shadow, waterfall, mist, or wet-rock mask generation
claiming complete Pacuare corridor imagery coverage
photoreal material extraction or texture derivation
remaining_blockers: Record terms/attribution and package policy for Sentinel COG use before committing any full-resolution bands or derived source drapes.
Perform windowed COG reads clipped to the Pacuare corridor and stitch all intersecting tiles before replacing the MODIS/GIBS drape.
Use SCL/QA bands and manual visual review to mask cloud, shadow, haze, water, canopy, wet rock, waterfalls, and mist.
Compare imagery date/season to rainfall and guide/outfitter observations before promoting river color, wetness, or flow-dependent visuals.
Keep thumbnails as review references only until full-resolution imagery and masks are approved.
review_decision: Attach public Sentinel COG thumbnails and tile metadata as the first higher-resolution Pacuare visual reference beyond MODIS/GIBS. They are useful for selecting source imagery, but not adequate for production drapes, masks, or photoreal materials.
source_catalogs: Copernicus Data Space OData product metadata
Element 84 / AWS Open Data Sentinel-2 L2A COG mirror

source_scene_context.selection_reason: January 19, 2025 was the lowest-cloud Sentinel-2 L2A candidate in the first expanded Copernicus metadata page for the Pacuare planning bounds.
